channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Canada wants new oil pipelines to avoid Trump tariffs; nobody wants to build them
item.description: Canada sends 90 percent of oil exports to U.S. refiners. The Canadian government would have to play a significant role in any project to build new oil pipelines in Canada to overcome regulatory, financial and political hurdles and activist opposition, industry experts said.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: DOGE subcommittee to examine US foreign aid spending
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene said the hearing comes as Americans are "shocked" to discover how their taxpayer dollars have been spent overseas.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: White House explains bruise on Trump's hand seen during Macron meeting
item.description: The White House says the bruise seen on the back of President Donald Trump's hand as he met French President Emmanuel Macron is from shaking people's hands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the Supreme Court of the United States and federal courts work and affect Texans
item.description: Here’s how federal courts help determine the fate of controversial issues, such as immigration laws, in Texas and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By María Méndez, Graphics By Yuriko Schumacher, KPRC 2

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon faces sentencing for threatening ex-Speaker McCarthy
item.description: A Montana man faces years in federal prison at his sentencing on Wednesday for threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration creates registry for immigrants who are in the US illegally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is creating a registry for all people who are in the United States illegally, and those who don't self-report could face fines or prosecution, immigration officials announced Tuesday. Everyone who is in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Public records disputes target of Nevada lawmaker's bill creating an ombudsman
item.description: A tug-of-war lies ahead as groups look to influence legislation designed to resolve disputes over access to public records.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police are investigating assault allegations against Republican US Rep. Cory Mills of Florida
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Police in Washington, D.C., are investigating assault allegations against Republican U.S. Rep. Cory Mills of Florida, but an official says the case has been sent back for further investigation and might never result in charges.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk’s DOGE moves will ‘turn out more Democrats than Obama': Carville
item.description: Carville told NewsNation that the Tesla billionaire will be "the greatest generator of Democratic turnout, ever, more than Barack Obama.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Cabinet members have already backtracked on some promises made before being confirmed
item.description: As they mustered support for their confirmations by the U.S. Senate, some of President Donald Trump’s appointees made statements from which they’ve already distanced themselves upon taking office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Zelenskyy offers to resign in exchange for peace or Ukraine’s NATO membership
item.description: “If it’s about peace in Ukraine, and you really want me to leave my position – I am ready to do that," Zelenskyy told reporters at a press conference in Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
